patterns_discovered: Covering queries with non-existent IDs across multiple database tables
Verifying graceful handling of impossible query conditions
Testing database operations with zero limit and large offsets
Ensuring updates/deletes on non-existent records do not throw errors
gotchas_discovered: Database might return different behaviors for non-existent IDs
Need to handle cases where queries can return empty arrays or undefined
Importance of testing impossible query conditions
Updates/deletes on non-existent records should be idempotent

approach_outcome.description: Test queries for non-existent IDs and other edge cases

recommendations: Maintain consistent null/empty result handling across all database interactions
Document expected behavior for queries with non-existent IDs
Consider adding more granular error handling for different query types
Implement similar comprehensive edge case testing for other database schemas
